question_title: Will ASML’s Q1 2026 results show total net sales strictly above €8.5 billion?
summary: ASML guided Q1 2026 total net sales at €8.2-8.9B, with the €8.5B threshold sitting at 42.9% of the guidance range (below midpoint of €8.55B). Analyst consensus from 18 analysts is €8.63B, €130M above threshold. ASML's Q4 2025 landed at 86% of guidance range, demonstrating a tendency to beat midpoint. Strong backlog (€38.8B) and AI-driven demand support revenue. However, revenue is lumpy due to high-value EUV system deliveries (~€370M each) — slippage of even 1-2 systems could swing revenue by several hundred million euros. Monte Carlo simulations across multiple distributional assumptions (uniform, normal, mixture) consistently produce probabilities in the 60-74% range, centering around 67%.

factors.description: Analyst consensus (18 analysts) at €8.63B is €130M above the €8.5B threshold, requiring a >1.5% revenue miss to resolve NO
ASML guidance range €8.2-8.9B puts threshold at 42.9% through range - below midpoint, so average execution clears it
Massive backlog of €38.8B and Q4 2025 bookings of €13.2B (double consensus) support strong near-term revenue pipeline
ASML Q4 2025 beat: actual €9.72B vs €9.57B estimate, landed at 86% of guidance range showing pattern of upside
AI capex cycle strong: Jensen Huang raised AI chip orders to $1T through 2027, TSMC capex at $52-56B for 2026
Revenue recognition lumpiness: EUV systems worth ~€370M each, 1-2 system delivery slippage could shift revenue by €370-740M
China revenue declining from 42% of Q3 2025 due to tightening export controls, reducing a major revenue stream
Macro/tariff uncertainty from US trade policy could dampen semiconductor equipment demand

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.67 (67% probability YES)**

Key reasoning:
- **ASML's own guidance** for Q1 2026 is €8.2-8.9B, with the €8.5B threshold sitting below the midpoint (€8.55B). Even average execution should clear it.
- **Analyst consensus** of €8.63B (18 analysts) sits €130M above threshold — ASML would need to miss consensus by >1.5% to fall below.
- **Strong demand fundamentals**: €38.8B backlog, massive Q4 bookings, surging AI capex.
- **Main downside risk**: Revenue is very lumpy. EUV systems cost ~€370M each, so slippage of 1-2 systems could materially reduce revenue. The threshold is only €50M below the guidance midpoint — not a large buffer.
- Monte Carlo simulations across multiple distributional models consistently produce 60-74% probability, centering around 67%.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will ASML’s Q1 2026 results show total net sales strictly above €8.5 billion?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42804)
[ASML reports €32.7 billion total net sales and €9.6 billion net income in 2025](https://www.asml.com/en/news/press-releases/2026/q4-2025-financial-results)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if ASML’s first official Q1 2026 financial results release reports total net sales strictly above €8.5 billion, as shown on ASML Investor Relations at https://www.asml.com/investors/financial-results
fine_print: Use the first official Q1 2026 release posted by ASML, not later revisions, transcript paraphrases, or third-party summaries. If ASML posts both a press release and a slide deck at the same time and the figures differ, use the headline financial results figure ASML presents for total net sales. Candidate Sources: ASML Financial Calendar https://www.asml.com/investors/financial-calendar
